findings.one_line: The law-class membership proof uses the marginal-overlap and gamma=0 strict-overlap conditions, but those assumption nodes are not in `depends_on`, so the membership claim is not reproducible from the declared parents alone.

The feasible upper-bound proof appeals to undeclared intermediate results (`lem:clipped-score-drift-bound`, `lem:clipped-score-variance-envelope`, `lem:feasible-master-bound`, `lem:clip-balance-exponent`) and also relies on clipped-propensity variance stability without declaring that assumption, so this node is not discharged by its current DAG.
The upper half of the headline rate bracket depends on `oeq:feasible-upper`; until that node is fully discharged, the two-sided rate characterization remains only partially proved.
